title: prunella vulgaris var. lanceolata
intro: Prunella vulgaris var. lanceolata, also known as lance-leaved self-heal or self-heal, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The health benefits of Prunella vulgaris var. lanceolata include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Prunella vulgaris var. lanceolata is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and can also be found in capsule or tablet form, making it a popular herbal supplement among those seeking natural remedies.
